claim: Cross-check of explorer claim: YOLO demand evidence schema, prompts, readiness contract, and tests distinguish project, external, user, and unknown evidence scopes with validation logic that is comprehensive but has enforcement gaps.

evidence.summary: VALID_EVIDENCE_SCOPES declared as Set(['project', 'external', 'user', 'unknown']) confirms four-scope taxonomy.
evidence.why: This is the canonical scope definition used throughout validation logic.

evidence.summary: DEMAND_EVIDENCE_RESULT_SCHEMA_DEFINITION documents scope requirement in evidence field description.
evidence.why: Schema contract explicitly states four-scope model and project evidence requirements.

evidence.summary: evidenceRecordScope() classifies evidence using explicit scope field first, then falls back to url/path/source heuristics.
evidence.why: Implementation shows scope classification prioritizes explicit declaration but allows inference from other fields.

evidence.summary: evidenceRecordHasProjectLocator() validates project evidence has non-URL path but does NOT validate the content was read from that path.
evidence.why: This validator checks path presence/format but not evidence provenance - it cannot detect web research cited with a fake project path.

evidence.summary: Validation rejects URLs in path field even when scope=project is declared, preventing obvious external mislabeling.
evidence.why: This catches explicit URLs in path field but does not catch external research cited without URL field.

evidence.summary: projectEvidenceBlockingIssues() blocks external-only scope but does NOT detect external research disguised as project scope with a path.
evidence.why: The validator only checks the explicit scope field; an agent can cite web research as scope=project by providing any project path.

evidence.summary: buildBoundarySnapshot() and diffBoundarySnapshots() detect file mutations using git or file walking with SHA-256 digests.
evidence.why: Boundary enforcement prevents agents from modifying project files but cannot verify evidence claims about what they read.

evidence.summary: Agent prompt instructs: 'External research tools may be available; mark external records as scope=external and do not use them as project facts.'
evidence.why: Prompt instruction depends on agent compliance; no runtime check prevents agents from violating this.

evidence.summary: Test 'external-only evidence cannot satisfy existing project facts' validates EXTERNAL_RESEARCH_EVIDENCE_REQUIRED blocker for explicitly external-scoped evidence.
evidence.why: Test confirms external scope is blocked, but does not test external research mislabeled as project scope.

evidence.summary: Test 'external URLs cannot be relabeled as project evidence' validates URL-in-path detection but does not test external research without explicit URL field.
evidence.why: Test catches URL-in-path mislabeling but not web research cited without URL field.

evidence.summary: PROJECT_EVIDENCE_PATH_REQUIRED blocker fires when scope=project declared but path/line missing; does NOT validate evidence actually came from that path.
evidence.why: This validates format but not provenance - the gap allows agents to cite external research with fake project paths.

evidence.summary: evidenceScopeErrors() validates each evidence record has valid scope value from VALID_EVIDENCE_SCOPES set.
evidence.why: Runtime validation checks scope is one of the four declared values.

evidence.summary: demandRequestsExternalResearch() and externalEvidencePresent() detect explicit external research requests and external-scoped evidence.
evidence.why: System validates external research requests produce external-scoped evidence, but cannot enforce separation if agents mislabel scope.

evidence.summary: Test 'cross-check PRD readiness requires every evidence agent result' validates explorer, cross-checker, verifier all required for cross_check policy.
evidence.why: Test confirms three-agent protocol enforcement in readiness contract.

assumptions: Agents comply with prompt instructions to keep external research separate from project facts
Agents do not fabricate project paths to cite external web research as project evidence
Agents actually read files they cite with project-scoped paths rather than guessing based on web research
Evidence with scope=project and a valid path was actually read from that file, not synthesized from external sources
risks: Agent could cite external web research (StackOverflow, MDN, vendor docs) as scope=project by providing any valid project path without URL field, bypassing validation
Agent could claim to have read a project file that it actually inferred from web search, with no runtime check to detect this mismatch
Readiness contract cannot distinguish genuine project file reads from web research cited with fabricated project paths
Evidence validation checks format (path exists) but not provenance (evidence actually came from that path)

result.notes: CONFIRMED: YOLO distinguishes four scopes (project, external, user, unknown) across schema, router, dispatch, and tests. VALIDATED: Scope classification, explicit declaration requirements, and readiness blockers all function as designed. VALIDATION GAPS: System relies on agent compliance to prevent external research from being cited as project facts. An agent can bypass validation by: (1) citing web research with scope=project and any valid project path, (2) omitting url field entirely while claiming to have read a local file, (3) fabricating evidence that passes format checks without actually reading the cited file. The validation logic checks scope format and path presence but cannot verify evidence provenance. Tests confirm external scope is blocked and URL-in-path is rejected, but do not test web research mislabeled as project scope with fake paths.
